title: Korean Crispy Gimbap
description: A delicious Korean dish that combines crispy rice paper sheets with chewy beef and fluffy rice. Perfect for a lazy Sunday afternoon.

ingredients.name: Beef (thinly sliced)
ingredients.notes: Low-sodium, brine-dried beef for optimal flavor.

ingredients.name: Gimbap rice paper sheets
ingredients.notes: Fresh or frozen, thawed according to package instructions.

ingredients.name: Butter
ingredients.notes: Unsalted, for greasing the rice paper sheets.

instructions: Preheat a non-stick skillet or griddle over medium heat.
In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, sugar, sesame oil, gochujang, salt, and black pepper.
In a separate bowl, whisk together egg and a pinch of salt.
Dip each gimbap rice paper sheet into the egg wash, coating both sides evenly.
Place a sheet of gimbap rice paper on a flat surface and brush one side with butter.
Sprinkle a thin layer of rice onto the center of the rice paper sheet, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Add a few slices of beef, some green onions, and a sprinkle of toasted sesame seeds on top of the rice.
Fold the bottom edge of the rice paper sheet up over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll into a tight cylinder.
Repeat with the remaining rice paper sheets and filling ingredients.
Heat the skillet or griddle over medium heat and cook the gimbap for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until crispy and golden brown.
Serve warm with pickled ginger and a side of kimchi, if desired.
Refrigerate and consume within 1 week — this preparation is not shelf-stable without an approved acid level and canning process.

Refrigerator pickle only: store in the fridge and consume within 1 week. For shelf-stable canning, add at least 2 tablespoons of vinegar (5% acidity) per cup of brine and follow a tested water-bath process.
